title: Vendor Performance KPIs for Quality Teams
author: B. Subramanian
excerpt: How to design vendor KPIs that give pharma quality teams inspection-ready, risk-weighted supplier oversight aligned with EU GMP, GDP and ICH Q9/Q10.
cover_image_prompt: A photorealistic clinical-luxury banner in a cool teal and cyan palette over deep navy, with a subtle green accent. Shallow depth of field captures a pharmaceutical quality analyst's hands reviewing a translucent supplier performance scorecard on a glass surface, faint data trend lines and gauge dials glowing softly out of focus behind. Soft natural light rakes across brushed-steel laboratory fixtures and a row of blurred sample vials, conveying precision, oversight and trust. Premium, calm, editorial composition, 4:3 aspect ratio, no text, no logos.

faqs.q: How many vendor KPIs should a pharma quality team track per supplier?
faqs.a: Fewer than most teams expect. A focused set of four to eight metrics across quality, data integrity, reliability and compliance is far more effective than a sprawling scorecard nobody trusts. Each KPI should tie to a specific decision you will make, and the number should be weighted by supplier criticality under ICH Q9, so your most critical suppliers receive deeper monitoring than low-risk ones.

faqs.q: What is the difference between leading and lagging vendor KPIs?
faqs.a: Lagging indicators, such as reject rates and confirmed complaints, measure harm that has already occurred, while leading indicators, such as change-notification compliance and audit-finding trends, help you anticipate problems before they cause a batch failure. A robust programme balances both. Relying only on lagging metrics means you are always reacting rather than preventing, which undermines the continual-improvement intent of ICH Q10.

faqs.q: Are vendor KPIs a regulatory requirement under EU GMP and GDP?
faqs.a: There is no rule prescribing a specific list of KPIs, but EU GMP Chapters 5 and 7 and the GDP guidelines require ongoing control and qualification of suppliers, and ICH Q10 expects supplier performance to feed management review. KPIs are the practical mechanism for evidencing that continuous oversight. Inspectors will expect to see that you monitor critical suppliers over time and act on the data, not merely that you audited them once.
